message:
fix(notifications): forward Cx through RateLimitedSender wrapper (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Applies the Cx-forward fix pattern from ticks 30-32 (Arc<dyn>,
UnifiedClient, ShardedWeztermClient) to the `RateLimitedSender`
NotificationSender wrapper. Without this override, the trait default
`send_with_cx` delegates to the wrapper's `send()` which loses cx at
the wrapper boundary — `inner.send_with_cx(cx)` is never reached.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/notifications.rs (+30 src)

   `impl<S: NotificationSender> NotificationSender for RateLimitedSender<S>`
   gains an explicit `send_with_cx` override that:

     1. Runs the same rate-limit check as `send()` (within_window
        computation, delivery emission on rate-limit hit).
     2. On the non-rate-limited path, calls
        `self.inner.send_with_cx(cx, payload)` instead of the ambient
        `self.inner.send(payload)` — threading caller cx through the
        wrapped sender's Cx-first path.

Impact: for notification chains that wrap a Cx-aware sender (e.g.
webhook with an asupersync HTTP client) in a rate limiter, caller
cancellation now propagates through both the rate-limit check and
the underlying transport send.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib notifications::: 46/46 pass
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. Concrete NotificationSender
impls (webhook dispatcher, desktop notifier) still use the default
send_with_cx (which delegates to their ambient send()) — those are
separate future slices that require their respective transports to
expose Cx-aware variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
